snap.caption: via Binance · today's change (from UTC 00:00) · RSI 14 / SMA 200 · funding USDT-perp

bottom.title: Possible short-term bottom
bottom.l1: · Historically, readings below 25 often coincide with short-term bottoms.
bottom.l2: · Zone to consider scaling into BTC/ETH (not all in). Alts may stay volatile.
bottom.l3: · Avoid chasing the downside. Prioritize risk management.

weak.title: Weakening trend
weak.l1: · Sentiment contracting. Trend momentum is weak.
weak.l2: · Only enter new trades on clear setups, at ~70% of usual size.
weak.l3: · Consider tightening stops on open positions.

neutral.title: No clear direction
neutral.l2: · Prioritize technical signals (trend/structure); sentiment is secondary.

chase.title: Don't chase
chase.l1: · Greed dominant — late entries have poor risk/reward.
chase.l2: · Take partial profits on open positions; trail stops up.
chase.l3: · Safer to wait for a pullback before new entries.

correction.title: Correction risk ↑
correction.l1: · Extreme greed — short-term correction risk rises sharply.
correction.l2: · Prioritize taking profit/hedging; hold off on new buys.
correction.l3: · Above 75 often sees a -10 to -20% correction within 1-2 weeks.

dominance.subtitle: BTC share of total market cap

btcStrong.title: BTC-led market · alts weak
btcStrong.l1: · Money rotating from alts back to BTC. Alts stay weak without their own catalysts.
btcStrong.l2: · Recommend higher BTC weight, lower alt weight in the portfolio.
btcStrong.l3: · Trade alts as short swings only, no trend-following.

btcLead.title: BTC lead · be selective on alts
btcLead.l1: · BTC trend leads. Stick to top-cap majors (ETH/SOL) for alts.
btcLead.l2: · A downturn in dominance would signal alt season.

balance.title: Balanced · sector rotation
balance.l1: · Money moving between BTC and alts. Specific sectors (L1/DeFi/AI) may show strength.
balance.l2: · Following volume-backed strong sectors works best.

altLead.title: Alt lead · alt season possible
altLead.l1: · Money moving from BTC to alts. Alt-season entry signal.
altLead.l3: · Can lower BTC weight and raise alt weight.

altSeason.title: Alt season underway
altSeason.l1: · Most Top 50 alts outperform BTC. Capital rotates fast.
altSeason.l3: · But a prolonged 75+ reading signals a late stage — review take-profit levels.

altLead.title: Alt lead
altLead.l1: · Over half of alts outperform BTC. Favorable for selective entries.
altLead.l2: · Focus on top-cap alts with volume.

neutral.title: Neutral · be cautious
neutral.l1: · No clear direction. Sector rotation possible.
neutral.l2: · Trade alts as short swings; avoid large bets.

btcSeason.title: BTC season
btcSeason.l1: · Most alts weak vs BTC. Alt volatility lower.
btcSeason.l2: · Recommend higher BTC weight. Trade alts short rather than buy-and-hold.

overheat.title: Korea overheated · don't chase
overheat.l1: · Upbit is 4%+ above global. Arbitrage selling pressure.
overheat.l2: · Chasing the Korean market is risky. Short-term correction possible.
overheat.l3: · Converges via global (Binance) catching up or Korea falling.

fomo.title: FOMO entry stage
fomo.l2: · Scale in and size down on new entries.

reverse.title: Reverse premium · possible bottom
reverse.l1: · Korea cheaper than global. Korean sell-off or USD strength.
reverse.l3: · Short-term bounce candidate — consider scaling in.

normal.title: Normal · in sync with global
normal.l1: · Within ±2% — Korea/global price gap is reasonable.
normal.l2: · Premium signal is meaningless. Prioritize other indicators (technical/news).

riskOn.title: Buy-side fuel building · risk-on
riskOn.l1: · Rising stablecoin supply = more capital entering the market.
riskOn.l2: · Favorable for risk assets. Good for buying BTC/alts.
riskOn.l3: · But there's a lag between issuance and actual buying.

drain.title: Liquidity draining · volatility ↑
drain.l1: · Falling stablecoin cap = money leaving the market.
drain.l2: · Volatility ↑, downward pressure. Consider trimming positions.
drain.l3: · Tighten stops and be conservative on new entries.

stable.title: Stable · no major capital flows
stable.l2: · Judge market direction from other indicators (sentiment/technical).

longOverheat.title: Long overheated · liquidation risk
longOverheat.l2: · Hold off on new longs. Short counter-trade candidate.
longOverheat.l3: · Signal is stronger if funding is also high positive.

longLead.title: Long lead · favorable for trend-following
longLead.l2: · But judge alongside funding rate and dominance.

shortOverheat.title: Short overheated · squeeze possible
shortOverheat.l1: · Short positions crowded. Short squeeze possible.
shortOverheat.l2: · Buy-favorable zone. Hold off on new shorts.

shortLead.title: Short lead · bounce candidate
shortLead.l2: · Bounce-trade candidate after a deep pullback.

balance.title: Balanced · no skew
balance.l2: · Refer to technical signals and funding rate first.

defi.footer: via DeFiLlama · 30d trend · rising = risk-on · falling = risk-off

strongRiskOn.title: Strong risk-on · altcoin-friendly
strongRiskOn.line1: · Strong capital rotation into DeFi. Favorable for buying risk assets.
strongRiskOn.line2: · Trend-following on major alts (ETH/SOL/AVAX) is favored.
strongRiskOn.line3: · A TVL spike on a specific chain signals strength in its token (e.g., SOL ecosystem).

mildInflow.title: Mild capital inflow
mildInflow.line2: · Some increase in alt allocation possible; keep sizing normal.

neutral.title: Flat · no signal
neutral.line1: · No major capital movement. Prioritize other indicators (sentiment, dominance).
neutral.line2: · Range trading is favored over directional trading.

deleveraging.title: Deleveraging starting
deleveraging.line1: · Early capital outflow. Volatility may rise.
deleveraging.line2: · Reduce alt sizing; be conservative on new entries.
deleveraging.line3: · Tighten stops and prepare for further downside.

strongRiskOff.title: Strong risk-off · high volatility
strongRiskOff.line1: · Risk-aversion mode. DeFi capital outflow underway.
strongRiskOff.line2: · Consider trimming open positions. Hold off on new entries.
strongRiskOff.line3: · Even short-term bounces carry low reliability.

flow.footnote: via CoinGecko · Alt mcap estimated from BTC.D

alt_season_entry.title: Alt season entry
alt_season_entry.body1: · New inflows (Stable ↑) + dominance ↓ + alt strength — capital goes straight to alts, bypassing BTC.
alt_season_entry.body3: · Can raise alt weight, lower BTC weight.
alt_season_entry.body4: · A break below 50% dominance is an even more aggressive signal.

btc_led_rally.title: BTC-led rally
btc_led_rally.body1: · Inflows (Stable ↑) + BTC rising faster than alts.
btc_led_rally.body2: · Early-cycle pattern — alts usually lag by 1–3 weeks.
btc_led_rally.body3: · Enter BTC-focused for now; target an alt rotation when dominance turns.

stables_deploying.title: Stables → risk assets
stables_deploying.body1: · Stablecoin cap falling + alt strength = sidelined capital converting to actual buys.
stables_deploying.body2: · Possible alt-season peak — trend still valid but short-term volatility ↑.
stables_deploying.body3: · Review take-profit lines; keep new entries to short swings.

rotation_alts_to_btc.title: Alts → BTC rotation
rotation_alts_to_btc.body1: · Alt cap ↓ + BTC flat-to-up — alt season ending signal.
rotation_alts_to_btc.body2: · Recommend trimming alt weight, raising BTC weight.
rotation_alts_to_btc.body3: · Keep alts to short trades until the next cycle.

liquidity_tightening.title: Liquidity tightening
liquidity_tightening.body1: · Stablecoin issuance ↓ + total cap flat — short on buying ammo.
liquidity_tightening.body2: · Higher volatility risk. Reduce new entry size.

deleveraging.title: Deleveraging · risk-off
deleveraging.body1: · Total cap ↓ + stablecoin cap ↓ — capital exiting the market.
deleveraging.body3: · Even short bounces have low reliability — don't catch the knife.

neutral.title: Wait · no major flow
neutral.body1: · All metrics within ±1–2% — no meaningful capital movement.
neutral.body2: · Judge market direction from other signals (sentiment, technicals).
neutral.body3: · Focus on scalp/day trades, avoid large bets.

cmpA.infoHeading: What data does the AI research look at?
cmpA.infoIntro: Objective data is pulled from Binance's public API, then the AI interprets that data to build scenarios. The AI does not invent prices — it judges only from objective numbers.
cmpA.bucket1Desc: Three timeframes are analyzed at once to see the big picture, setup, and trigger together.
cmpA.bucket1Item1: HTF (higher TF) — market bias (whether it goes up or down)
cmpA.bucket1Item2: MTF (mid TF) — setup location (where to enter)
cmpA.bucket1Item4: ATR — market volatility (whether the stop width is appropriate vs. noise)
cmpA.bucket1Item5: VWAP — volume-weighted average price (institutional entry baseline)
cmpA.bucket2Desc: Objective indicators that find where price stalls and reverses.
cmpA.bucket2Item2: FVG (fair value gap) — price tends to come back to fill it
cmpA.bucket2Item3: Order Block — the last candle just before a strong reversal
cmpA.bucket2Item4: Liquidity zone — where stop orders cluster (reversal after a sweep)
cmpA.bucket2Item5: Volume Profile (POC) — volume-center price level (short-term magnet)
cmpA.bucket2Item6: Weekly Volume Profile — higher-timeframe magnet (reference for targets)
cmpA.bucket3Desc: Who is more positioned in, and which side the real buy/sell flow is on.
cmpA.bucket3Item1: Order Book — buy/sell walls, spread, imbalance
cmpA.bucket3Item2: Order Flow — actual market buys vs. sells, large trades
cmpA.bucket3Item3: Funding rate + 24h trend — direction of crowding / unwinding
cmpA.bucket3Item4: Open Interest (OI) + hourly change — new entries vs. liquidations
cmpA.bucket3Item5: Top trader long/short ratio — whale positioning (information asymmetry)
cmpA.bucket4Desc: The big picture beyond the individual coin + market mood.
cmpA.bucket4Item1: 24h quote — current price, change, high/low, volume
cmpA.bucket4Item2: BTC dominance — BTC market-cap share (essential for alt trading)
cmpA.bucket4Item3: DXY (dollar index) — usually inverse to BTC, macro risk-on/off
cmpA.bucket4Item4: Fear & Greed Index — overall market sentiment (extremes are contrarian signals)
cmpA.pipelineStep1Desc: Fetches the above data in parallel from Binance/CoinGecko (in code, deterministic)
cmpA.pipelineStep2Desc: Picks 1 of 5 strategies (trend pullback / breakout / range fade / reversal / wait)
cmpA.pipelineStep3Desc: Derives entry, stop, target, and trigger within the chosen strategy
cmpA.captureNotFound: Could not find an area to capture.

cmpB.compactTitle: Historical volatility bootstrap (N={n}) · no direction assumed. Expected value {exp}R
cmpB.fullTitle: A probability structure from randomly resampling the historical volatility distribution (bootstrap, N={n}). Direction (drift) is assumed to be 0 — this isn't a price forecast but the probability created by "distance to target vs. distance to stop + volatility".
cmpB.grabNoSweepSubtitle: The LLM chose this strategy, but the detection module found no recent sweep. Confirm the scenario's rationale via the card's trigger.
cmpB.grabBullishSubtitle: Briefly broke the prior low then recovered — where big players liquidated sell stops. A long-bias signal.
cmpB.grabBearishSubtitle: Briefly pierced the prior high then recovered — where big players liquidated buy stops. A short-bias signal.
cmpB.squeezeSubtitle: {crowded} positions crowding — a candidate to enter {reverse} targeting a forced-liquidation cascade. Time limit 12–24 hours.
cmpB.driveSubtitle: Strong directionality in the first 30–60 min after the {session} open — follow that direction. Exit before the session close.

analyze.title: Using AI Research
analyze.description: When it's best to analyze and how to read the results. It depends on your trading style and the market session.
analyze.styleDayWhen: At the 1H candle close · session transitions
analyze.s1Intro: Market data is most stable right after a candle closes. Analyzing in the middle of an in-progress candle picks up noise, and results often flip.
analyze.avoidNews: 30 min before and after major releases — FOMC, CPI, Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP), etc.
analyze.avoidVolatility: The 30 min right after a volatility spike — analysis reliability drops sharply
analyze.s2Title: How to read the analysis results
analyze.s2Intro: The analysis comes in 6 sections. Read top to bottom: from the big structure → to the narrow entry conditions.
analyze.read1Desc: Whether the higher timeframe (HTF) and lower timeframe (LTF) are aligned. Same direction is 'aligned', conflicting is 'mixed'.
analyze.read1Tip: Trend trading is only valid when aligned + a strong trend. If mixed, switch to range trading.
analyze.read2Desc: POC (volume center), VAH/VAL (value area), and prior highs/lows. The points price is drawn to like a magnet.
analyze.read2Tip: Your entry should be near these levels. Far from a level means chasing risk.
analyze.read3Desc: Buy/sell delta, order book imbalance, and funding rate. The short-term pressure direction.
analyze.read3Tip: When flow agrees with the scenario direction, confidence ↑. When it conflicts, you need a wait or a strong trigger.
analyze.read4Desc: Each scenario has an entryZone, invalidation (stop), target, and staged entry prices + weights.
analyze.read4Tip: If entryType is 'pending', price must reach the entryZone before entering. 'immediate' means you can enter now.
analyze.read5Desc: An A–D grade per scenario. It scores R:R, BTC alignment, range avoidance, volume, psychology, and more.
analyze.read5Tip: A is a good trade, B conditional, C not recommended, D no-trade. C or below: declining entry is recommended.
analyze.read6Desc: Items where the scenario deviates from standards. Insufficient stop width, R:R below target, etc.
analyze.read6Tip: Shown in an amber box. The more items, the more you should strengthen your own judgment before entering.
analyze.s3Title: Automatic scenario tracking — historical hit rate
analyze.s3Intro: When you save an analysis, its scenarios are automatically registered in the tracking system. Regardless of whether you actually entered, the system tracks price reaching the levels every 5 minutes and accumulates hit-rate data.
analyze.hitRateBody: Next to the AI confidence in the analysis result, "Historical hit rate X% (Y wins Z losses, +N.NR avg)" is shown automatically. The color is green/yellow/red by grade. Samples are summed over the last 30 days per (symbol × strategy) combination.
analyze.hitRateSampleNote: With fewer than 3 samples, "Insufficient sample" is shown
analyze.mis1Wrong: The AI recommended it, so I can just buy
analyze.mis1Right: A scenario is a market hypothesis. The entry decision must pass through the grade + quality issues + your own money management.
analyze.mis2Wrong: Enter all of the multiple scenarios
analyze.mis2Right: Only 1 main scenario per analysis. Secondary scenarios are alternatives for when the main one is invalidated.
analyze.mis3Wrong: Missed the entry price, so just chase it
analyze.mis3Right: If the market price moves more than 50% of the stop width past your entered entry price, the guard blocks it. Wait for the next setup.
analyze.mis4Right: Once per candle close is enough. Excessive re-analysis causes decision paralysis.
analyze.principle1: Analyze once right before entry + re-check at each candle close
analyze.principle2: If re-analysis shows no big change, proceed as is
analyze.principle3: Do not re-enter the same scenario more than twice
analyze.principle4: A scenario is a hypothesis, not a prediction
analyze.faq1Q: How many credits does one analysis use?
analyze.faq1A: One per run. The balance is deducted right before the analysis starts. If it fails, it's refunded.
analyze.faq2Q: How long are analysis results kept?
analyze.faq2A: Kept permanently. You can reopen them on the analysis history page and run the scenarios as-is.
analyze.faq3Q: Can I attach a chart image?
analyze.faq3A: Yes. It's used as supplementary context. However, the actual decision is based on the data (snapshot).
analyze.faq4Q: What if the analysis comes back as 'wait'?
analyze.faq4A: Mixed indicators or no signal. Re-analyze after the next candle close, or move to a different symbol.

arbitrage.title: Delta-Neutral Kimchi Premium Arbitrage
arbitrage.description: With a long spot position on Upbit + a short futures position on Binance, your coin price exposure is hedged to zero, and whenever the kimchi premium crosses ±the threshold, automatic rebalancing accumulates profit purely from the premium's oscillation.
arbitrage.s1Emphasis1: Long spot on Upbit + short futures on Binance
arbitrage.s1IntroMid: to start. The long spot and short futures offset each other, so even as the coin's absolute price rises and falls, PnL is zero (delta neutral). Profit comes solely from the oscillation of the
arbitrage.s1Emphasis2: kimchi premium (the price gap between the two exchanges)
arbitrage.s1IntroTail: , and whichever way the premium widens, a cycle captures the spread.
arbitrage.plusCycleBody: Premium reaches ≥ +threshold → sell spot on the pricier Upbit + cover the short on the cheaper Binance. Both exposures shrink together, keeping it delta neutral, with USDT profit equal to the price gap. It moves 25% of inventory each time.
arbitrage.minusCycleBody: Premium reaches ≤ −threshold → buy spot on the cheaper Upbit + add to the short on the pricier Binance. Both exposures grow together, keeping it delta neutral. The same capital can profit in both directions.
arbitrage.s2Title: Entry decision — using the expected-return ranking
arbitrage.s2Intro: It monitors 26 coins every 5 minutes and backtests a 7-day premium time series to show each coin's expected return. It is not a simple cycle count but an empirical PnL estimate that also reflects inventory depletion + coin price movement.
arbitrage.read1Desc: Cumulative PnL from simulating the actual cron logic as-is over the last 7-day time series, assuming $1000 of exposure.
arbitrage.read1Tip: A large positive = a good entry. If negative, the loss from coin price movement exceeds the cycle profit.
arbitrage.read2Desc: The number of cycles where inventory actually moved during the backtest. Not the number of simple threshold crossings.
arbitrage.read2Tip: If simple cycles ≫ effective cycles, the coin's inventory depleted quickly. Risk of one-sided skew.
arbitrage.read3Desc: The ratio of +/− cycles. A 50/50 balance is ideal; over 90% on one side is a red warning.
arbitrage.read3Tip: A coin whose average premium is near 0 produces evenly balanced cycles in both directions → sustainable.
arbitrage.read4Desc: The coin / USDT distribution across exchanges at the end of the backtest. A 50/50 balance is healthy; over 90% on one side is red.
arbitrage.read4Tip: A signal that inventory depleted over the past 7 days. The same pattern may repeat after you enter.
arbitrage.s3Intro: Threshold = the absolute premium value that triggers a cycle. Lower means more frequent cycles with smaller profit per cycle; higher is the opposite.
arbitrage.threshold03Trait: Balanced (recommended for high-volatility alts like DOGE)
arbitrage.threshold10Trait: For high-volatility coins. Rare cycles but large profit
arbitrage.s3Footnote: Fixed costs: 0.04% fee × both sides + 0.02% slippage × both sides = 0.12% breakeven. Below 0.2% risks a loss.
arbitrage.s4IntroLead: When you click enter, a modal shows the backtest summary + a delta-neutral note.
arbitrage.s4IntroTail: offset each other so coin price exposure is 0 — even if price falls, the cycle profit stays intact.
arbitrage.deltaNeutral1: Upbit long spot PnL ≈ the opposite of Binance short PnL → coin price moves are offset
arbitrage.deltaNeutral2: The only profit source is the oscillation of the premium (the cross-exchange price gap)
arbitrage.deltaNeutral3: Shows a 7-day price range for reference (this move is offset by the hedge)
arbitrage.pos1Label: Upbit long spot / Binance short futures boxes
arbitrage.pos1Body: — the quantity, cash, and net assets of each leg
arbitrage.pos2Body: — the ratio of the Upbit long to the Binance short. 50/50 keeps it delta neutral; net exposure should be near 0 to be normal
arbitrage.pos3Body: — the coin quantity movable in each +/− direction + its USD value. If one direction is near 0, a depletion warning
arbitrage.pos4Body: — every cycle in chronological order (direction/premium/amount moved/profit)
arbitrage.warn1: Coin price exposure is hedged — but the funding rate (the cost of holding the short) is a residual risk
arbitrage.warn2: If the premium stays stuck in one direction for a long time, inventory depletes → no more profit is generated
arbitrage.warn3: On expiry, auto-liquidation or manual liquidation
arbitrage.warn4: Currently simulation only (real kimchi premium arbitrage is not possible under Korean foreign-exchange law)
arbitrage.faq1A: Coins near the top of the expected-return ranking + with a direction distribution near 50/50 + a 50/50 balanced final inventory. Typically, coins whose average premium is near 0 with a large standard deviation are ideal.
arbitrage.faq2Q: Why is profit small even with many cycles?
arbitrage.faq2A: If only one direction repeats, inventory skews to one side and there's no more movable amount left. You can check the degree of depletion via the simple-cycles vs effective-cycles difference.
arbitrage.faq3A: The cron automatically liquidates by selling spot on Upbit + covering the short on Binance. Coin price moves are offset by the hedge, so the cumulative premium-cycle profit determines the final PnL.
arbitrage.faq4Q: Can I hold multiple positions at once?
arbitrage.faq4A: Yes. Different coins can be held simultaneously. Each position uses margin equal to 2 × notional.

game.title: Using the Price Prediction Game
game.description: Predict the open→close direction of a 1-minute or 3-minute candle. A tool for building market intuition through fast turnover.
game.phase1Body: Enter your predicted direction (up/down) and amount.
game.phase2Body: Just before betting closes. New bets are blocked.
game.phase3Body: Waiting for the next candle's open to be confirmed.
game.phase4Body: A dotted entry-price line (the next candle's open) is shown on the chart.
game.phase5Body: If the close is above the entry price, up wins; if below, down wins.
game.s1IntroLead: Betting is a game of guessing the open→close direction of the
game.s1IntroTail: . The entry-price reference is the next candle's open, not the moment you bet. This approach removes the unfairness caused by your betting moment.
game.exampleMid: is the entry price → judged by the 14:02 candle's
game.exampleTail: . Close > open is a win, < open is a loss.
game.betTieBody: — when open = close, the bet is refunded (rare)
game.faq1Q: The price already moved right after I bet — isn't that unfair?
game.faq1A: No. The entry price is not the price at the moment you bet but the next candle's open. Everyone who bets on the same candle gets the same entry price.
game.faq2Q: How long is the betting window?
game.faq2A: Up to 5 seconds before the current candle closes. After that, it automatically moves to the next candle's betting phase.
game.faq3Q: Can I bet on several candles in a row?
game.faq3A: You can, but only 1 bet is active at a time. The in-progress game must settle before the next bet.
game.faq4Q: Where do I check game results?
game.faq4A: The history sidebar on the left of the game page. They're also reflected in the ranking automatically.

results.title: Using My Results · Review
results.description: Every trade you enter is accumulated automatically. This explains how to read the stats and how to find patterns.
results.tab1Desc: Lists of open / pending limit / closed trades. 4 KPIs (today's R, open, unrealized, exposure).
results.tab2Desc: 4 hero KPIs (cumulative R, win rate, Profit Factor, streak) + the equity curve + breakdowns by grade/direction/exit reason.
results.tab3Desc: Game / trading / combined categories × daily / weekly / monthly / all-time. Rewards every Monday at 00:00 KST.
results.s2Title: How to read the key metrics
results.metric1Body: The sum of realized R across all closed trades. If it accumulates positively, you have an edge. With few trades, variance is high (30+ recommended).
results.metric2Body: The share of winning trades. Insufficient on its own — high win rate + small R can still lose. Read it together with Profit Factor.
results.metric3Body: Total profit ÷ total loss. ≥1.5 is stable. Below 1.0 is a long-term loss. 2 or more is excellent.
results.metric4Body: The current consecutive win/loss count. 3+ consecutive losses calls for a forced break (psychological money management).
results.metric5Body: A chronological cumulative-R curve. Green above the 0 line, red below. The single peak/trough trade is shown in the header.
results.s3Intro: A tool for quickly finding where you make money and where you lose it.
results.bd1Body: — if you're + only at grade A and - at C or below, the grade system is trustworthy. Reduce your C·D entries.
results.bd2Body: — cumulative R for long vs short. A skew to one side means market bias or your own bias.
results.bd3Body: — stop vs target vs manual. Many manual exits may mean rule violations. If the stop ratio is too high, revisit your stop criteria.
results.s4Intro: Distributed automatically every Monday at 00:00 KST. Run separately for the game, trading, and combined categories.
results.scoreCriteriaBody: vUSDT PnL (= realized profit). It's the size of cumulative profit, not the number of trades — one big hit can beat many small trades.
results.faq1Q: Can I edit or delete a trade?
results.faq1A: Only open trades can be liquidated manually. Closed trades cannot be deleted, to preserve statistical integrity.
results.faq2Q: When do I get AI coaching?
results.faq2A: Press the "AI Review" button on the trade detail page to generate coaching for that trade. 1 AI credit is deducted.
results.faq3Q: Where do I see backtest results?
results.faq3A: Backtesting is currently on hold. The preserved work is on GitHub under the archive/backtest-v1 tag and will be enabled when work resumes.

trading.instantSettleBody: Press the "Settle now" button on the trade journal page to check immediately without waiting 5 minutes.

trading.mm4Body: — when nearing it, an automatic -2 point grade deduction

trading.pnl1Body: — net R after fees. At 0.84R or more, the fee burden is cleared.
trading.pnl2Body: — auto-refreshed every 10 seconds (CORS-bypassing Binance ticker API).
trading.pnl3Body: — the closer it is, the more imminent the outcome.

The Service is a tool that helps members check, against objective data, the trading decisions they make on their own, and does not recommend the purchase or sale of any particular asset.
terms.p4b: The scenarios, grades, and comments presented by the AI are for members' reference only and do not guarantee returns. All trading decisions and outcomes rest with the member's own judgment and responsibility.
terms.h5: Article 5 (Provision and Change of the Service)
terms.p5li1: The Service is, in principle, provided 24 hours a day, year-round.
terms.p5li2: However, it may be temporarily suspended in the event of force majeure such as system maintenance, replacement, or failure, communication outages, or natural disasters.

terms.p6li2: If a person misappropriates another's identity or provides false information, registration may be refused or terminated thereafter at the Service's discretion.
terms.p7li1: Members must comply with these Terms and applicable laws.
terms.p7li2: Members are responsible for managing their own account and password and may not transfer or lend them to any third party.
terms.p7li3: Members may not reproduce, transmit, distribute, or commercially exploit the Service's content without authorization.
terms.h8: Article 8 (Payment and Cancellation of Paid Plans)
terms.p8li1: Payment, cancellation, and refunds for Paid Plans are subject to the separate Refund Policy.
terms.p8li2: A member may cancel a Paid Plan at any time, and billing stops from the next billing cycle after cancellation.

terms.p9li2: The Service may terminate the service agreement after prior notice if a member materially violates these Terms (e.g., identity theft, unauthorized distribution of content, or system attacks).
terms.p10li1: The Service may provide an auxiliary feature ("Live Trading Integration") that uses an exchange API key voluntarily registered by the member to transmit the member's own orders to the exchange. Live Trading Integration is executed only when the member completes a confirmation step directly on screen.
terms.p10li2: When issuing an API key, members must comply with the following.
terms.p10li2sub1: Never enable withdrawal permission (Enable Withdrawals). The Service blocks registration of keys that have withdrawal permission.
terms.p10li2sub2: Where possible, use exchange-side security options such as IP whitelisting.
terms.p10li2sub3: The API key is the member's asset, and responsibility for its management and periodic rotation rests with the member.
terms.p10li3: The Service stores registered API keys encrypted with AES-256-GCM and transmits no orders to the exchange other than upon the member's explicit order request. However, synchronization of automatic stop-loss/take-profit closing for positions the member has entered (querying order status and canceling the unfilled opposite-side order) is deemed agreed to by the member's use of live trading mode.
terms.p10li4: The Service provides live-trading safeguards (blocking upon reaching a cumulative daily -2R, per-trade exposure limits, blocking duplicate entries on the same coin, etc.), but these are auxiliary measures only and cannot prevent all losses. Members use live trading at their own risk.
terms.p10li5: The Service is not responsible for outcomes arising from exchange outages, slippage, partial fills, forced liquidation, API rate-limit excess, key permission changes, and the like.
terms.p10li6: This Live Trading Integration feature may be restricted or discontinued without prior notice in accordance with the Virtual Asset User Protection Act (effective July 19, 2024) and changes to related regulations.
terms.p11li1: The AI research, grades, and comments provided by the Service are for learning and decision-support purposes and do not guarantee accuracy, completeness, or timeliness. The Service is not responsible for losses arising from a member trading in reliance on the Service.
terms.p11li2: The Service is not responsible for damages caused by natural disasters, failures of external APIs (exchanges, LLMs, etc.), or causes attributable to the member.

privacy.metaDesc: An overview of the types of personal data VECTA collects, uses, and retains, and how it is processed.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: VECTA (the "Service") values members' personal data and complies with applicable laws including the Personal Information Protection Act. This policy explains what personal data the Service collects, uses, retains, provides, and destroys.
privacy.h1sub2: Automatically collected during use of the Service
privacy.h1sub2li1: Trade inputs (coin, entry price, stop-loss, target, account, risk, etc.)
privacy.h1sub2li2: AI research results and trade outcome records
privacy.h1sub2li3: Access IP, browser information, access time (cookies/session)
privacy.h1sub3li1: Payment identifier (issued by the payment processor; no direct storage of card numbers)
privacy.h1sub3li2: Payment receipt information (business information when a tax invoice is requested)
privacy.h1sub4li1: Telegram chat ID, Discord webhook URL (when notifications are used)
privacy.h2li1: Member identification, account management, identity verification
privacy.h2li2: Generating AI research results and storing trade records
privacy.h2li3: Processing Paid Plan payments and refunds
privacy.h2li5: Statistical analysis to improve the Service (in a form that cannot identify individuals)
privacy.h3li1: Member information: until the member closes their account. Permanently deleted immediately upon closure and removed from backups within 30 days.
privacy.h3li2: Payment records: 5 years under the Act on Consumer Protection in Electronic Commerce
privacy.h3li3: Access logs: 3 months under the Protection of Communications Secrets Act
privacy.h4p: The Service does not provide members' personal data to third parties. The following are exceptions, however.
privacy.h4li1: When the member has consented in advance
privacy.h4li2: When required by law or upon a lawful request by an investigative agency
privacy.h5p: For stable operation, the Service entrusts some tasks to external processors.
privacy.h5li1: Supabase Inc. — database and user authentication (storage location: AWS Tokyo/Seoul regions)
privacy.h5li3: Anthropic PBC — AI research API (zero data retention enabled; analysis request data is not used for training)
privacy.h5li4: Payment processor (planned: Stripe, Toss Payments, etc.) — payment processing
privacy.h6p: Members may exercise the following rights at any time.
privacy.h6li1: Request to access, correct, or delete personal data
privacy.h6li3: Withdraw consent and close the account (via the settings screen or email request)
privacy.h6li4: Right to data portability (download your own data in a processable form)
privacy.h7li2: Per-user data isolation via database Row-Level Security
privacy.h7li3: HTTPS applied across the entire path
privacy.h7li4: Minimization of access rights and retention of access logs
privacy.h8p: The Service uses cookies to maintain login sessions and improve the user experience. Members may refuse cookie storage in their browser settings, but in that case some features such as login may be limited.
privacy.h9p: For inquiries about personal data processing, please contact us below.
privacy.h10p: This policy may be revised in accordance with changes in law or the Service's policies, and notice will be given from 7 days before the effective date.

refund.metaDesc: An overview of the refund criteria and procedures for VECTA Paid Plans.
refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: VECTA (the "Service") applies the following refund criteria to protect members' reasonable reliance. This policy complies with the Act on Consumer Protection in Electronic Commerce and the Content Industry Promotion Act.
refund.h1: 1. 100% refund within 7 days (including simple change of mind)
refund.h1p: If requested within 7 days of the payment date, the full payment amount is refunded without asking for a reason. The following cases are excluded, however.
refund.h1li1: When 50% of the monthly AI research count has already been used
refund.h1li2: When more than half of that month's analysis count has already been used
refund.h1note: Even in the above cases, a full refund applies if there was a service outage or material defect.
refund.h2li1: Monthly subscription: the Service is provided normally until the end of the billing cycle, and pro-rated refunds are, in principle, not provided.
refund.h2li2: Annual subscription: 50% of (remaining months × monthly-equivalent amount) is refunded (with a cancellation penalty deducted).
refund.h2li3: If normal use was impossible for 7 days or more due to a service outage, a pro-rated refund is guaranteed.
refund.h3: 3. Cases where refunds are not available
refund.h3li1: When the service agreement is terminated due to a member's material breach of the Terms
refund.h3li2: Re-refund of a payment already refunded
refund.h3li3: Payments more than 90 days old (the deadline for raising an objection)
refund.h4li1Post: or hello@alphagate.app with your sign-up email, payment date, and refund reason.
refund.h4li2: After verifying your identity within 1–2 business days, we'll let you know whether a refund is available.
refund.h4li3: After refund approval, the refund is issued to the payment method (card) within 3–7 business days. Depending on the card issuer, it may be reflected in the next billing cycle.
refund.h5sub1: Example 1 — Simple change of mind within 7 days
refund.h5sub1p: Standard monthly ₩15,000 paid on May 1 → refund requested on May 5 → full refund of ₩15,000
refund.h5sub2: Example 2 — One month of use after payment
refund.h5sub2p: Pro monthly ₩95,000 paid → refund requested after using 60% of the analysis count → not eligible for a pro-rated refund as it is after 8 days. A pro-rated refund applies, however, if normal use was impossible due to a service outage.
refund.h6p: If you want to stop the next payment, you can cancel auto-renewal from the settings screen without requesting a refund. You can use the Service normally through the current billing cycle, and billing stops from the next payment.
refund.h7p: If you object to the refund handling, you may apply for mediation through the Korea Consumer Agency (Consumer Dispute Mediation Committee).
refund.contactPre: For refund inquiries, please write to the
refund.contactPost: or hello@alphagate.app. We reply within 24 hours on business days.

q1.q: Can I really start for free?
q1.a: Yes. The Free plan is free forever and you can sign up without a credit card. Each month you get 5 AI analyses plus unlimited trade execution, trading journal, and performance analytics.

q2.a: Only the monthly AI research count differs. Trade execution, the trading journal, performance analytics, and AI review comments are unlimited on every plan.

q3.a: No. They refill on your billing date each month and any remaining count does not carry over. Pick the plan that matches your average trading frequency.

q4.q: Can I get a refund after paying?
q4.a: Full refunds are available within 7 days of payment. Email us at hello@alphagate.app and we'll process it within 1–2 business days.

q5.a: Yes. You can upgrade or downgrade anytime. Upgrades are prorated and your new count applies immediately. Downgrades take effect from the next billing cycle.

q6.q: Does the AI recommend buying or selling?
q6.a: No. VECTA only lays out scenarios and invalidation conditions — it never recommends a specific buy or sell. All trading decisions and outcomes are your own responsibility.

q7.a: All data is isolated per user via Supabase Row-Level Security. Other users can't access it even if there's a code bug, and you can delete your account and all your data at any time.

features.metaDescription: AI research / trade execution / trading journal + AI review / performance analytics — take a closer look at VECTA's four core features.
features.heroBody: Four core features. From pre-entry analysis to post-exit review, all in one platform.

analyze.title: Read the market with objective data
analyze.body: Just enter a symbol and style — VECTA auto-collects 12+ data points from the public Binance API and presents 1–3 scenarios. The AI never invents prices; it only interprets real data.
analyze.bullet1: 3-stage pipeline: data collection (code) → strategy classification (LLM) → scenario generation (LLM)
analyze.bullet2: Per-style stop/target standards enforced (scalp / day / swing / position)
analyze.bullet3: Entry, stop, target, and trigger conditions auto-derived for each scenario
analyze.bullet4: Chart visualization + HTF/MTF/LTF toggle + PNG download
analyze.bullet5: Analysis history saved automatically + filters + pagination

trade.title: Enter like an exchange, get a grade back
trade.body: Take an AI scenario and enter the entry, stop, target, account, risk, and leverage just like an exchange order screen. An A/B/C/D grade and an action recommendation appear instantly.
trade.bullet1: Auto-detects chasing entries, unconfirmed candles, BTC direction conflicts, and mid-range entries
trade.bullet2: Risk-%-based auto position sizing + recommended leverage calculation
trade.bullet3: Effective risk-reward shown after deducting 0.12% in fees
trade.bullet4: Auto-suggests recommended leverage when margin is exceeded + 1-click apply
trade.bullet5: Large long/short buttons + automatic % on price + size chips (25/50/Max)

journal.title: Connect outcomes to decisions
journal.body: The grade, score, and market checks at entry are preserved permanently. Enter your actual R after exit and the AI evaluates your decision and execution, auto-generating a coaching comment.
journal.bullet1: Market snapshot saved permanently at entry (reproduce the state from that moment)
journal.bullet2: Result entry: exit price, realized R, exit reason, mistake tags, notes
journal.bullet3: AI review: decision evaluation + execution evaluation + improvements for the next trade

dashboard.title: Shows you where you're losing
dashboard.body: Auto-aggregates only closed trades to visualize stats by grade, by mistake, and by time. Learn your trading patterns from objective data, not emotional hindsight.
dashboard.bullet1: Average R / win rate / trade count by grade — A-grade vs C/D-grade comparison
dashboard.bullet2: Cumulative P&L by mistake tag + auto-highlight of your "most frequent losing pattern"
dashboard.bullet3: Track over time with a monthly cumulative R graph
dashboard.bullet4: AI research history integrated too — how analysis connected to decisions

analyze.caption: Analysis result — scenario cards
analyze.trigB: Rejection at 79,200 + drop on rising volume
analyze.now: ⚡ Now: hold off, waiting for scenario A trigger

journal.caption: AI review comment
journal.p1Before: Your entry was reasonable at grade B (score 6). That said,
journal.p1Strong: taking profit at 80% of the way to target
journal.p2Strong: entering only after the trigger candle's close is confirmed
journal.p2After: should move your average R up a notch.

how.metaDescription: How VECTA validates your entry decisions — a 4-step cycle of AI research → trade execution → trading journal → performance analytics.
how.heroBody: Trade with the same flow every time and emotion has no room to creep in. VECTA automates everything from entry to review in a 4-step cycle.
how.pipelineBody: The AI never invents prices. It takes objective data and only interprets it.
how.dataBody: It's not just looking at the chart. VECTA collects objective data simultaneously from Binance, CoinGecko, Yahoo Finance, and alternative.me for a comprehensive judgment.

analyze.title: AI research
analyze.body: Just enter a symbol and trading style. The AI reads the market structure from objective data and presents 1–3 scenarios.
analyze.bullet1: Binance real-time data (candles, order book, fills, funding, OI) + ATR, VWAP, BTC dominance, DXY, and Fear & Greed combined
analyze.bullet2: The Strategy Agent picks 1 of 5 strategies (pullback / breakout / range / reversal / wait)
analyze.bullet3: Entry, stop, target, trigger conditions, and invalidation criteria derived for each scenario

trade.title: Trade execution
trade.body: Enter the scenario from the AI research like an exchange. An A/B/C/D grade instantly judges whether to enter.
trade.bullet1: Auto-detects chasing entries, unconfirmed candles, BTC direction conflicts, and mid-range entries
trade.bullet2: Risk-%-based auto position sizing + recommended leverage calculation
trade.bullet3: Effective risk-reward shown after deducting 0.12% in fees
trade.bullet4: D-grade means no entry, A-grade means enter as is, B/C come with a caution

journal.title: Trading journal
journal.body: Save your entered trades and record the actual outcome after exit. Compare the entry evaluation and the actual result on one screen.
journal.bullet1: Market snapshot saved permanently at entry (reproduce the market state from that moment)
journal.bullet2: Enter exit price, realized R, exit reason, mistake tags, and notes
journal.bullet3: AI review: decision evaluation + execution evaluation + next-trade improvements auto-generated

dashboard.title: Performance analytics
dashboard.body: Auto-aggregates closed trades by grade, by mistake, and by time. Understand your trading patterns from objective data.
dashboard.bullet1: Average R / win rate / trade count stats by grade
dashboard.bullet2: Cumulative P&L by mistake tag — your most frequent losing pattern auto-highlighted
dashboard.bullet3: Track improvement over time with a monthly cumulative R chart
dashboard.bullet4: AI research history integrated too — what you analyzed and how you acted

collect.title: Data collection
collect.body: Parallel fetch from 12+ sources including Binance Futures, Spot, CoinGecko, Yahoo Finance, and alternative.me. Swings, FVG, OB, POC, ATR, and VWAP auto-calculated.

classify.title: Strategy classification
classify.body: The collected data is passed to the LLM to pick 1 of 5 strategies. It outputs direction (long/short), confidence, and even the reasons rejected strategies were dropped.

scenario.title: Scenario generation
scenario.body: Generates 1–3 scenarios only within the chosen strategy's scope. Entry, stop, and target are derived to match objective data levels.

price.title: Multi-TF candles
price.body: Three timeframes at once (HTF bias / MTF setup / LTF trigger)

structure.title: Swings · FVG · Order Block
structure.body: Auto-detects highs/lows, unfilled gaps, and strong reversal candles

flow.title: Trade flow (aggTrades)
flow.body: Last 500 market fills, buy vs sell ratio, large trades

vol.title: ATR · VWAP
vol.body: Volatility metric + session volume-weighted average price

dom.title: BTC dominance · DXY
dom.body: BTC market-cap share + dollar index (inverse correlation)

fng.title: Fear & Greed Index
fng.body: Overall market sentiment (extremes are contrarian signals)

rls.title: Row-Level Security
rls.body: Per-user row access is enforced at the PostgreSQL level. Other users' data can't be accessed even if there's a code bug.

data.title: Objective data first
data.body: We never ask the LLM for prices directly. We pull real data from the public Binance API and the LLM only interprets it. Price hallucination is impossible.

storage.title: Transparent storage
storage.body: Every analysis snapshot and trade result is stored in Supabase under your account only. Delete anytime.

notify.title: Notification Settings
notify.description: Receive alerts via Telegram or Discord for D-grade trades, losing streaks, completed AI reviews, scenario price alerts (only for scenarios you registered with 🔔 in analysis results), and good-time-to-analyze reminders. Register once and all alerts come to the same channel.
notify.discordHint: Paste the URL created under Discord Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
notify.analysisAlertHint: Sends a "now is a good time to analyze" alert at the times you select (KST). Pick times that suit your style. If none are selected, nothing is sent.
notify.analysisAlertFootnote: A Telegram or Discord channel must be connected to send.

golden.label: Golden Hours
golden.note: London·New York overlap — peak liquidity, trends form. Highest analysis confidence.

dead.label: Dead Zone
dead.note: US close to Asia open — lowest liquidity, frequent whipsaws. Short-term trading not advised.

active.label: Active Session
active.note: Europe·US session — healthy liquidity. Good for trend and breakout analysis.

quiet.label: Quiet Session
quiet.note: Asia session — low volatility, frequent ranges. Moderate confidence on short-term signals.

funding.label: Funding settlement — wait briefly
funding.advice: ±10 min around settlement is volatility noise. It's cleaner 10 minutes later.

optimal.label: Optimal entry
optimal.advice: London·New York overlap — best trend and liquidity. The best time to enter.
optimal.adviceWeekend: Liquidity is good but it's the weekend — gap risk, so size down.

good.label: Good entry
good.advice: Europe·US session — healthy liquidity. Good for trend and breakout entries.

caution.label: Enter with caution
caution.advice: Asia quiet — frequent ranges and many fake breakouts. Be careful.
caution.adviceWeekend: Weekend — low liquidity, range and gap risk. Be cautious with new entries.

avoid.label: Entry not advised
avoid.advice: Dead zone (US close to Asia open) — lowest liquidity, whipsaws. Entry not advised.

tight.actionable: Analysis=entry together · ready to act now ({label})
tight.later: Analysis=entry together · hard to enter now → analyze + enter after {clock} KST

moderate.now: You can analyze and enter now, but {label}
moderate.later: Analyze now and plan → good entries start from {clock} KST

loose.now: Analysis is for planning · liquidity is also good for entry right now
loose.later: Analysis is for planning · recommended entry at {clock} KST (when liquidity is good)

link.none: Little timing impact · scale in over several days

dow.midweek: Tue·Wed·Thu — most stable trends, the best trading days
dow.monday: Monday — high volatility as weekend gaps get absorbed (opportunity and risk)
dow.friday: Friday — positions wind down from the afternoon; be cautious with new swings
dow.weekend: Weekend — low liquidity; watch for Sunday-night spikes

dca.title: Spot DCA bot
dca.subtitle: Buys more when cheap, less when pricey — accumulates automatically (spot)
dca.universeNote: Only large-cap majors can be accumulated. Buying more on the way down only works on assets that survive.
dca.tiltNote: Always buy, but tilted - 2x when cheap, 1x neutral, 0.5x expensive. Waiting for cheap only meant up to 2.6 years without a buy, which no one sustains.
dca.noPlans: No plans yet. Pick an asset on the left to create one.
dca.confirmDelete: Delete the {sym} plan? Executed buys stay in your journal.
